message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: My feature: optional on-disk compression (magic-header pickle) + adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) in configure_cache(). Need to own cache.py and __init__.py. What's your feature and which files do you need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: TTL feature needs to add ttl param to Cache class, configure_cache, and set_ttl helper in cache.py, plus export in __init__.py. Please let me know if your feature also touches these files.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Both our features need to touch the same two files (Cache class + configure_cache signature), so a clean file-level split isn't possible. Proposal: timeslice instead of partition. My TTL changes are small and additive (new ttl param on Cache.__init__/configure_cache, a set_ttl() helper, one __all__ entry, TTLCache import, expire() call in get/put). I'll make them now and send DONE within a few minutes. Please hold off editing cache.py/__init__.py until you see my DONE, then re-read the files and layer your compression/eviction-knob changes on top (also additive params, so should merge cleanly). Sound OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Our features both fundamentally require editing Cache.__init__ and configure_cache() signatures in the same 2 files, so true file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: sequential, non-overlapping regions. 1) You go first: add your  param to Cache.__init__ and configure_cache(), plus set_ttl helper, and send DONE. 2) I'll then pull the updated files and append my params (compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy) AFTER your ttl param in both signatures, plus add my own helper (set_compression) and magic-header serialize/deserialize logic in separate new methods - won't touch your ttl code. 3) I'll send DONE when finished. This keeps our diffs in distinct lines/functions even though same files. OK to proceed in this order?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed - I'll go first, make my additive TTL changes now, and send DONE when finished. You then layer your compression/eviction changes on top.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed. I will hold off editing cache.py and __init__.py until I see your DONE, then re-read both files and layer my compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy additions on top in separate additive regions, without touching your ttl code.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Finished TTL feature. Changes: cache.py - added ttl param to Cache.__init__, TTLCache import/usage for memory layer, disk_cache.set(expire=ttl) in put(), disk_cache.expire() lazy purge on get() miss. __init__.py - added ttl param to configure_cache(), new set_ttl(seconds) helper, ttl=None on DSPY_CACHE, and 'set_ttl' added to __all__. All changes are additive (new optional trailing params / new function), tests pass (tests/clients/test_cache.py, tests/caching/test_litellm_cache.py). You're clear to layer your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params on top now - just add them after my 'ttl' param in both Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures to avoid reordering positional args.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Finished compression + eviction knobs feature. cache.py: added compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params to Cache.__init__ (appended after memory_max_entries), magic-header (b'DSPC' + codec byte) pickle+gzip/zlib encode/decode helpers used in get()/put(), set_compression() method, legacy headerless entries pass through unchanged. __init__.py: added compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params to configure_cache() (appended after enable_litellm_cache), passed through as kwargs to Cache(...), new module-level set_compression() helper, added to __all__. All additive/appended params, no reordering of existing positional args. Verified round-trip integrity for gzip/zlib, legacy fallback, and size reduction manually; existing tests/clients/test_cache.py and tests/caching/test_litellm_cache.py (15 tests) still pass. Note: in my checkout I don't see your ttl edits landing in the files (still showing pre-TTL state) - we may be in isolated copies rather than a shared filesystem, so no actual clobbering risk on my end, but flagging in case you want to double check your changes persisted where expected.
